entries.description: Intergalactic espionage. Oh and some of the characters have superpowers.
entries.title: Star Wars Episode IV: A New Hope

entries.description: Best of the trilogy? Yep.
entries.title: Star Wars Episode V: Empire Strikes Back

entries.description: Fitting ending to a memorable trilogy.
entries.title: Star Wars Episode VI: Return of the Jedi

entries.description: Time travel done right.
entries.title: Groundhog Day

entries.description: Vibrant, exciting and over the top.
entries.title: Knives Out

entries.description: Frivolous yet gripping with an immaculate dressing.
entries.title: The Grand Budapest Hotel

entries.description: Culturally enlightening? Probably not. Culturally additive? Definitely yes.
entries.title: The Darjeeling Limited

entries.description: Immersive despite an outrageous premise.
entries.title: Ghostbusters

entries.description: Tom Cruise  + a well constructed time travel plot = an action packed, tense and visually pleasing viewing that makes you work.
entries.title: Edge of Tomorrow

entries.description: Makes for a fun viewing despite but with questionable perspectives on display
entries.title: The Social Network

entries.description: Complex in its construction and unnerving until the very end. A true masterpiece.
entries.title: Memento

entries.description: Hard to believe this was filmed as opposed to drawn by a meticulous artist. Light hearted and engaging.
entries.title: Moonrise Kingdom

entries.description: Conveyor of several deep insights masquerading as a frivolous story about foxes. Masterpiece #2 for the year.
entries.title: Fantastic Mr. Fox

entries.description: A roller coaster in its ability to play with your emotions at will. Charming in its ability to disorient you.
entries.title: The Royal Tenenbaums

entries.description: Progressively makes you feel uneasy.
entries.title: Bad Education

entries.description: Suspenseful all the way.
entries.title: The Thing

entries.description: Action Sci-fi to the core.
entries.title: The Terminator

entries.description: “I’ll be back” indeed. And better than before.
entries.title: Terminator 2

entries.description: Despite a questionable plot line, credit where credit is due immersing you in a world with dinosaurs.
entries.title: Jurassic Park

entries.description: Clinical execution all round.
entries.title: Dunkirk

entries.description: Unnerving throughout. Gut wrenching on some occasions.
entries.title: The Shining

entries.description: Charming, engaging and visually appealing.
entries.title: Isle of Dogs

entries.description: There is something intangible about Thurman in a Tarantino move. A string of memorable and unique set pieces the effortlessly forms a cohesive whole.
entries.title: Kill Bill: Volume 1

entries.description: Has the tough task of filling in some big shoes. Doesn’t quite achieve that, but is a great viewing nonetheless.
entries.title: Kill Bill: Volume 2

entries.description: Well executed and unsettling at different occasions.
entries.title: Full Metal Jacket

entries.description: Thoroughly succeeds in playing you in an alien world via phenomenon attention to detail.
entries.title: Dune

entries.description: I have no idea why just happened but I still enjoyed it.
entries.title: The French Dispatch

entries.description: A dazzling display with a fresh take on a well trodden path.
entries.title: Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse
